mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 140045k in Nov 2014 reflects post-crisis recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-crisis expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPI) rises above 2.5% annualized, real rates turn negative, eroding bond yields and forcing capital into equities as a real-return hedge, producing positive SPX returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% in Nov 2014, CPIAUCSL at 237.642 in Oct 2014
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of 60d forward returns, or mechanism fails to outperform single-series FEDFUNDS model by > 5% Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 2% signals weakening consumer demand and signals equity downside risk, producing negative SPX returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance revisions cascade through earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS shocks produce negative 5d returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1% month-over-month indicates accelerating manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20d returns at r > 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window while payroll growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates below 100k, labor market deterioration signals recession risk and produces negative SPX returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS growth < 100k month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.8% in Nov 2014, PAYEMS at 140045k
failure_condition: Joint deterioration signal fails to predict negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in logistic model.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1000k units annualized signals robust residential investment and forward consumer wealth effects, which drive positive equity returns over 20 trading days through multiplier effects on construction employment and materials demand.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or HOUST > 1000k fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 1.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on future corporate cash flows, producing negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5% and remains elevated for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% in Nov 2014, well below tightening threshold
failure_condition: Tightening episodes produce negative 60d returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand that supports equity earnings revisions upward, producing positive SPX returns over 20 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16164.1B in Q3 2014, RSAFS at 449282M in Nov 2014
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Joint demand signal fails to predict positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression model.
